content.text:
<user_query>
You are a reviewer for this repo commit review. Review requested commits 75a393ead and e2a5037b4 focusing on files humanoid-gym/scripts/train.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/scripts/fuyao_train.sh, humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_ppo.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/ppo/rollout_storage.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/iron_turbo/core.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/iron_turbo/distributed_utils.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_autoencoder_env.py, humanoid-gym/humanoid/utils/task_registry.py. Assess objective alignment, constraints, sequencing/cherry-pick risks, missing validation commands, and regressions, using commit diffs only. Return key high/medium/low risk findings with exact file:line references where possible and any hidden assumptions.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>

</agent_skills>

High-risk findings:

1. [High] Startup break: AMP runner imports missing policy modules unconditionally
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py:45-46`
```44:50:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py
from .amp_discriminator import AMPDiscriminator
from .utils import NormalizerTorch
from ..ppo.actor_critic import ActorCritic
from ..ppo.actor_critic_heightmap_embed import ActorCriticHeightmapEmbed
from ..ppo.actor_critic_cross_attention import ActorCriticCrossAttention
```
- Because these imports execute at module import time, importing `AMPOnPolicyRunner` fails immediately if those modules are not present in the repository state, preventing training startup before task/algorithm config selection.
- This creates a hard gating dependency not reflected in changelog/constructor wiring and violates robustness for non-cross-attention deployments.

2. [High] `ActorCritic.evaluate` return-contract mismatch breaks default policy path
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_ppo.py:155-157`, `184-187`, `249-250` call `evaluate(...)` as tuple-returning
```156:249:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_ppo.py
value, _ = self.actor_critic.evaluate(aug_critic_obs)
...
last_values, _ = self.actor_critic.evaluate(aug_last_critic_obs)
...
value_batch, latent = self.actor_critic.evaluate(aug_critic_obs_batch, masks=masks_batch, hidden_states=hid_states_batch[1])
```
- Base policy implementation still returns only a value:
```245-248:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/ppo/actor_critic.py
def evaluate(self, critic_observations, **kwargs):
    latent = self.env_factor_encoder(critic_observations)
    value = self.critic_body(latent)
    return value
```
- If policy class is default `ActorCritic` (as in many env configs), this raises unpacking runtime errors (ValueError/TypeError) in `act/compute_returns/update`.

3. [High] `BaseAMPLoader` API drift vs R01 loader/env call sites can crash init
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py:55-65` removed `amp_scene_tag_ratio` from constructor.
```52:65:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py
class BaseAMPLoader:
    def __init__(
        self,
        device,
        time_between_frames,
        motion_dataset,
        motion_dataset_version,
        motion_file_names,
        preload_transitions=False,
        num_preload_transitions=1000000,
        amp_scene_tag_lists=None,
    ):
```
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_motion_loader.py:34-45` still forwards `amp_scene_tag_ratio`.
```22:45:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_motion_loader.py
def __init__(..., amp_scene_tag_lists=None, amp_scene_tag_ratio=None):
    super().__init__(..., preload_transitions, num_preload_transitions, amp_scene_tag_lists, amp_scene_tag_ratio)
```
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py:101-112` calls the loader with `amp_scene_tag_ratio` from env cfg.
```101:112:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
self.amp_loader = r01_amp_loader_factory(... )(
    ...,
    add_amp_scene_tags=...,
    amp_scene_tag_lists=...,
    amp_scene_tag_ratio= self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_ratio if hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio') else None,
)
```
- Result: `TypeError` at loader construction in affected R01 configs.

4. [High] Removed rigid-link getter APIs are still called by R01 env reset path
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py` no longer defines torso/foot rigid-state getters in the inherited API:
```735:791:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py
def get_root_vel_batch...
...
def get_frame_scene_tags_batch...
```
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py:816`, `827`, `838` call removed methods directly:
```816:838:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
torso_pos, torso_quat, torso_lin, torso_ang = self.amp_loader.get_torso_rigid_state_batch(frames)
l_pos, l_quat, l_lin, l_ang = self.amp_loader.get_left_foot_rigid_state_batch(frames)
r_pos, r_quat, r_lin, r_ang = self.amp_loader.get_right_foot_rigid_state_batch(frames)
```
- This path has no `hasattr` guard and is unconditional in `_reset_link_states_amp`, so this is a guaranteed runtime failure once those methods are absent.

Medium-risk findings:

5. [Medium] ONNXRuntime provider device is hardcoded to CUDA 0 in autoencoder env
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_autoencoder_env.py:266-270`, `290-294`
```266:270:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_autoencoder_env.py
self.fpn_model = ort.InferenceSession(
    fpn_path,
    providers=['CUDAExecutionProvider', 'CPUExecutionProvider'],
    provider_options=[{'device_id': 0}, {}],
)
...
self.decoder_model = ort.InferenceSession(
    depth_decoder_path,
    providers=['CUDAExecutionProvider', 'CPUExecutionProvider'],
    provider_options=[{'device_id': 0}, {}],
)
```
- In distributed runs each rank usually maps to a different local rank GPU; forcing `device_id=0` can cause contention or missing-device failure on non-zero ranks.

6. [Medium] `fuyao_train.sh` distributed launch is effectively single-host only
- `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh:117-124`
```117:124:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh
RDZV_PORT=${RDZV_PORT:-$((10000 + RANDOM % 10000))}
python -m torch.distributed.run \
    --nnodes="${nnodes}" \
    --nproc_per_node="${nproc_per_node}" \
    --rdzv_endpoint="localhost:${RDZV_PORT}" \
```
- This conflicts with multi-node intent (`--nnodes`) and will not work reliably beyond local-node orchestration.

7. [Medium] `Accelerate` is now hard required on the training script code path
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/scripts/train.py:50-54`
```50:54:humanoid-gym/humanoid/scripts/train.py
from humanoid.algo.iron_turbo.distributed_utils import get_distributed_seed
from accelerate import Accelerator
...
```
- For environments that invoke training outside `fuyao_train.sh`, missing dependency now fails startup even when non-distributed mode is intended.

8. [Medium] Checkpoint schema changed (`amp_normalizer` key rename) breaks resume compatibility
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py:460-467,509-514`
```460:467:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py
"amp_normalizer_state_dict": self.alg.amp_normalizer.state_dict(),
...
self.alg.amp_normalizer.load_state_dict(loaded_dict['amp_normalizer_state_dict'])
```
- Older checkpoints using previous key name will fail to load unless migration code is added.

Low-risk findings:

9. [Low] `h5py` import is swallowed, then used later without hard precondition
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_autoencoder_env.py:87-90`, `1046`
```87:90:... try import h5py ... except ImportError: import subprocess
...
1046:1047:with h5py.File(file_path, "w") as f:
```
- If `h5py` unavailable and `record_ae_data` path is exercised, runtime error occurs in `save_data` path; behavior is non-obvious.

10. [Low] `e2a5037b4` legged-robot fix is in the right direction and aligns with objective
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py:235-238`
```235:238:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py
if self.cfg.env.send_timeouts:
    self.extras["time_outs"] = self.time_out_buf
```
- This fix removes stale `time_outs` propagation for GAE and is a valid fix for the intended bug.

Objective alignment & sequencing/cherry-pick risk summary:
- The distributed plumbing is largely wired through runner/training/util modules, and objective direction is consistent (`distributed` mode in `train.py`, distributed init in `task_registry`, and launcher changes in `fuyao_train.sh`).
- However, there are critical integration blockers that make the changes non-runnable as a unit:
  - missing-import blocker (`amp_on_policy_runner`),
  - policy API mismatch (`evaluate` unpacking),
  - R01 loader constructor and method-surface incompatibilities.
- This is therefore a high-risk sequence: `75a393ead` should not be treated as drop-in without also landing the missing policy modules and loader API restoration/back-compat fixes.

Missing validation commands / hardening gap:
- No explicit validation sequence was added for:
  - import-time smoke test of `humanoid.scripts.train` + `AMPOnPolicyRunner` in clean env,
  - distributed startup sanity (`1 node x N ranks`),
  - backward-compat checkpoint load from pre-existing checkpoints,
  - R01 env import with scene-tagged configs.
- Given the severity of the API/import regressions above, these checks are important before merge.

Assumptions to make explicit:
- Are `ActorCriticHeightmapEmbed` and `ActorCriticCrossAttention` expected to be included in the same commit set but currently missing from this workspace?
- Are R01 configs with `amp_scene_tag_ratio` still in active use? If yes, loader interface compatibility must be restored.
- Is the `distributed` launch expected to support multi-node? If yes, `torch.distributed.run` rendezvous host binding must be updated.
